sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see clearly what happened to the right panel:
Initial right panel:
- Red shape with white border (rows 16-22)
- Blue shape with white border (rows 32-39)
Current right panel:
- Red shape WITHOUT white border (rows 17-21) - shifted up by 1 row
- Blue shape WITHOUT white border (rows 34-38) - shifted up by 2 rows
So clicking on a shape removes its white border and shifts it. But wait, the shapes have changed positions too. Let me look at the exact positions:
Initial red: rows 16-22, with white border
Current red: rows 17-21, no white border (shifted up 1 row from the inner part)
Initial blue: rows 32-39, with white border  
Current blue: rows 34-38, no white border (shifted up 2 rows from the inner part)
Now let me think about the left panel changes:
Initial left:
- Blue square at rows 20-23, cols 20-23
- Horizontal bar: gray+red+dark red at rows 30-33
- Checkered at rows 34-37
Current left:
- Checkered at rows 20-23, cols 20-21 (next to yellow box)
- Horizontal bar: gray+red+dark red at rows 30-33 (same as initial!)
- Blue at rows 34-37
So the blue square and checkered pattern swapped positions on the left panel. The red bar stayed the same.
Wait, but I clicked red twice and blue once. Let me trace through all the changes more carefully:
1. Initial state
2. Click red (first time): red bar split, checkered moved up, blue stayed
3. Click blue: blue moved down to where checkered was
4. Click red (second time): red bar recombined, blue and checkered swapped
So after 3 clicks (red, blue, red), the left panel has:
- Blue and checkered swapped from initial
- Red bar back to normal
- Right panel: both shapes lost white borders
Hmm, so clicking red twice seems to toggle the red bar between split and combined states. And clicking blue once moved the blue square. 
Wait, I need to think about this differently. 
I'm tracking the color swaps and position changes across multiple clicks. The red bar appears to toggle between split and combined states when clicked, while the blue square and checkered pattern exchange positions. The right panel shapes consistently lose their white borders after being clicked. I'm noticing the red bar's behavior is inconsistent across clicks—it split during the first red click but recombined during the second. The blue and checkered elements seem to swap positions, though the exact mechanism isn't clear yet. I should systematically test clicking each shape to understand the underlying pattern, starting with the blue shape to see if it produces a predictable transformation.
